records.ety: [L. gerundium, fr. gerere to bear, carry, perform. See Gest a deed, Jest.]

defs: A kind of verbal noun, having only the four oblique cases of the singular number, and governing cases like a participle.
In Modern English, the -ing form of a verb, when functioning as a noun; as, running is good for the heart.
A verbal noun ending in -e, preceded by to and usually denoting purpose or end; -- called also the dative infinitive; as, “Ic hæbbe mete t etanne” (I have meat to eat.).
